expectations.text: A0: Agent produced both output files with real content
expectations.evidence: quality-scenarios.md (131 lines, 7 scenarios) and analysis.md (61 lines) both present with substantive content.

expectations.text: A1: Scenarios use ATAM format (Source, Stimulus, Environment, Artifact, Response, Response Measure)
expectations.evidence: All 7 scenarios use table format with all 6 ATAM fields: Source, Stimulus, Environment (some scenarios), Artifact, Response, Response Measure. Note: Some scenarios are slightly non-standard (QS-6 Response Measure has an OR condition rather than a single threshold), but all 6 fields are present.

expectations.text: A2: Diverse test types — at least 3 different types, not all fitness-functions
expectations.evidence: No test type field on any scenario. The scenarios do not classify themselves as unit-test, integration-test, load-test, etc. The analysis mentions QS-6 could be 'an automated fitness function' but this is a recommendation, not a classification. The tradeoff matrix table exists but does not assign test types.

expectations.text: A3: Style fitness functions NOT duplicated — no scenarios for structural invariants already covered by style FFs
expectations.evidence: All 7 scenarios focus on runtime behavior (latency with/without audit, audit completeness, crash recovery, degraded store). None test structural invariants like service boundaries or DB sharing. This eval's focus on tradeoffs naturally avoids structural duplication.

expectations.text: A4: Tradeoffs identified — at least 1 explicit tension/conflict between characteristics
expectations.evidence: The entire output is structured around the performance vs audit-logging tradeoff. The analysis documents the core conflict explicitly with a tradeoff matrix, decision points table, severity assessment, and detailed layer-by-layer analysis of how the conflict manifests. Very strong tradeoff identification.

expectations.text: A5: Summary table present — table listing all scenarios with test types
expectations.evidence: There is a 'Tradeoff Matrix' table listing scenarios with Performance/Audit columns, but it does not include test types. No summary table with test type classification exists.

expectations.text: A6: Response measures are CONCRETE — numbers, thresholds, or specific observable outcomes
expectations.evidence: All measures are concrete: 'p95 response time < 30ms' (QS-1), 'p95 response time < 50ms' (QS-2, QS-3), '100% of API calls have a matching audit record; zero gaps' (QS-4, QS-5), 'p95 < 50ms AND 100% local audit records written' (QS-7). QS-6 uses an OR threshold which is intentionally ambiguous to force a decision — still concrete.
